## Implementation Summary

### What Was Built
`WarehouseService` — a TypeScript service class for fetching warehouses by ID and updating warehouse capacity, following the same structure as `ProductService`.

### Files Created / Modified

| Path | Action | Purpose |
|------|--------|---------|
| `.tmp/warehouses/warehouse-repository.ts` | created | `Warehouse` entity interface + `WarehouseRepository` contract |
| `.tmp/warehouses/warehouse-service.ts` | created | `WarehouseService` with `getWarehouse` and `updateCapacity` |
| `.tmp/warehouses/warehouse-service.test.ts` | created | Jest unit tests covering all 3 acceptance criteria |

---

## Acceptance Criteria Coverage

| Criterion | Status | Test | Notes |
|-----------|--------|------|-------|
| AC1: `getWarehouse` returns warehouse when ID exists | ✅ implemented | `AC1: returns the warehouse when the ID exists` | Delegates to `repo.findById`, returns the entity directly |
| AC2: `getWarehouse` throws `WAREHOUSE_NOT_FOUND` when ID missing | ✅ implemented | `AC2: throws WAREHOUSE_NOT_FOUND when the ID does not exist` + `AC2: error is an AppError instance` | Throws `AppError("WAREHOUSE_NOT_FOUND", ...)` with message containing the ID |
| AC3: `updateCapacity` mutates capacity and calls `repo.save` | ✅ implemented | `AC3: updates the warehouse capacity and persists it` + `AC3: updateCapacity throws WAREHOUSE_NOT_FOUND when warehouse does not exist` | Reuses `getWarehouse` internally so missing-ID guard is automatic |

---

## Tests — 5/5 passing

| File | Covers | Type |
|------|--------|------|
| `warehouse-service.test.ts` | AC1 — found warehouse returned | unit |
| `warehouse-service.test.ts` | AC2 — not-found throws `WAREHOUSE_NOT_FOUND` | unit |
| `warehouse-service.test.ts` | AC2 — error is `AppError` instance with ID in message | unit |
| `warehouse-service.test.ts` | AC3 — capacity mutated, `save` called with updated entity | unit |
| `warehouse-service.test.ts` | AC3 (edge) — `updateCapacity` on missing ID throws, `save` never called | unit |

---

## Deviations

None. Implementation follows the `ProductService` pattern exactly.

---

## Confidence Assessment

```
confidence:
  level: 100
  high_confidence_areas:
    - Repository interface shape (matches ProductRepository pattern)
    - AppError reuse from shared/app-error.ts
    - getWarehouse delegation in updateCapacity (reuses guard, avoids duplication)
    - All 5 tests green
  low_confidence_areas: []
  assumptions:
    - Warehouse.capacity is mutable (no immutability constraints in spec)
    - No persistence adapter needed — repo is the boundary (injection by caller)
```
